articles.headline: Rice hails England's 'frightening' attacking substitutes
articles.description: Declan Rice reacts to England's 3-0 win over Costa Rica in their final friendly ahead of the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

images.name: Rice hails England's 'frightening' attacking substitutes
images.caption: Declan Rice reacts to England's 3-0 win over Costa Rica in their final friendly ahead of the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

articles.headline: England sign off WC prep with 3-0 win as Rice, Gordon, Watkins score
articles.description: Returning Declan Rice set England on course for a morale-boosting 3-0 victory against Costa Rica after a heavy thunderstorm in Orlando delayed their final World Cup warmup match by an hour.

images.name: Declan Rice [1296x729]
images.caption: Declan Rice opened the scoring for England shortly after 10 minutes played.

images.name: Tuchel: England can't be World Cup favourites
images.caption: Thomas Tuchel says England can't be favourites because there are "proven winners" at the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

articles.headline: Burley questions if Portugal's Martinez would drop Ronaldo
articles.description: Craig Burley debates whether Roberto Martinez should drop Cristiano Ronaldo amid growing scrutiny over his place in Portugal's starting lineup.

images.caption: Craig Burley debates whether Roberto Martinez should drop Cristiano Ronaldo amid growing scrutiny over his place in Portugal's starting lineup.

articles.headline: Argentina wins as Messi scores in cameo in front of 88k in Alabama
articles.description: Argentine legend Messi, who is working his way back from muscle fatigue ahead of the start of the FIFA World Cup, came on in the 70th minute and scored from the penalty spot right after in his team's 3-0 win over Iceland.

article.headline: Argentina celebrate World Cup with fans in win over Panama
article.description: Argentina beat Panama 2-0 in a friendly on Thursday with goals from Thiago Almada and Lionel Messi amid a festive atmosphere in Buenos Aires.
article.linkText: Monumental rocks as Argentina revel in WC win

images.name: Argentina celeb Monumental_230323 [1296x729]
images.caption: Argentina players hold up replica World Cup trophies in front of their fans at Monumental Stadium in Buenos Aires.

article.story:
Argentina beat Panama 2-0 in a friendly on Thursday with goals from Thiago Almada and Lionel Messi amid a festive atmosphere in their first game in front of their home fans after winning the World Cup in December.

Lionel Scaloni picked the same starting lineup from the World Cup final win over France as they looked dominant from the start, but could not break through the Panamanian defensive wall until late in the second half.

- Messi milestone tracker: Records continue to fall for the Argentine

Atlanta United FC's Almada opened the scoring in the 79th minute with his first international goal after capitalising on a rebound following a free kick by Messi that hit the left post.

Captain Messi finally got his 800th career goal and doubled the lead in the 89th minute with a brilliant free kick that went into the top right corner to excite the more than 80,000 fans chanting his name at the Monumental Stadium.

The party got started after the final whistle, with family members and children joining Messi and his Argentina teammates on the pitch with fireworks exploding in the background and fans cheering on their champion side, who hoisted replica World Cup trophies.

"I always dreamed of this moment, celebrating with you," an emotional Messi told the crowd. "To come to my country and lift a Copa America, the Finalissima and the greatest of all the trophies, the World Cup.

"I know this is our day to celebrate, but I don't want to forget all the teammates who were here before, who also put everything on the field to achieve this and we were very close before. They deserve all the recognition of the Argentine fans because they always did their best for these colors."

A tearful Scaloni added: "Eternal gratitude to these players. Football belongs to them, and without them we wouldn't have won the World Cup.

"Everyone who wears this shirt gives their all and sometimes the results don't come. But this time we got it and it's incredible."

Argentina will continue their World Cup winners' homecoming with a friendly against Curacao on Tuesday -- when Messi can score his 100th goal for his country -- while Panama face Costa Rica in the CONCACAF Nations League.

keyEvents.text: First Half begins.
Kevin Galván (Panama) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
Substitution, Panama. Omar Browne replaces Ronaldo Córdoba.
Second Half begins Argentina 0, Panama 0.
Substitution, Panama. Víctor Medina replaces Cristian Quintero.
Substitution, Argentina. Lautaro Martínez replaces Julián Álvarez.
Substitution, Argentina. Lisandro Martínez replaces Nicolás Otamendi.
Substitution, Argentina. Thiago Almada replaces Alexis Mac Allister.
Substitution, Argentina. Paulo Dybala replaces Ángel Di María.
Substitution, Argentina. Leandro Paredes replaces Enzo Fernández.
Edgardo Fariña (Panama) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
Substitution, Panama. Martín Morán replaces Irving Gudiño.
Substitution, Panama. Gilberto Hernández replaces Edgardo Fariña because of an injury.
Substitution, Panama. Michael Casazola replaces Kevin Galván.
Substitution, Argentina. Marcos Acuña replaces Nicolás Tagliafico.
Substitution, Panama. Miguel Camargo replaces José Murillo.

Goal!  Argentina 1, Panama 0. Thiago Almada (Argentina) left footed shot from the left side of the six yard box to the bottom right corner. Assisted by Leandro Paredes   following a set piece situation.
Miguel Camargo (Panama) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
Goal!  Argentina 2, Panama 0. Lionel Messi (Argentina) from a free kick with a left footed shot to the top right corner.
Second Half ends, Argentina 2, Panama 0.
